quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in place by flapping their wings in a figure-eight pattern up to 80 times per second. These tiny acrobats have excellent memories and can remember every flower they've visited.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals in the Pacific Northwest are more social and playful than we once thought. Learn how scientists are discovering their personalities and communication styles right here in the Salish Sea.

medium_read.title: The Library of Alexandria: What Really Happened
medium_read.teaser: The ancient Library of Alexandria wasn't destroyed in one dramatic fire, but slowly declined over centuries. Discover the real story of this legendary center of learning and what we lost when it faded away.

long_read.title: The Hidden World of Old-Growth Forests
long_read.teaser: Ancient forests like those in Olympic National Park create ecosystems that take centuries to develop. This beautiful exploration reveals how these forests communicate, support countless species, and why they're so irreplaceable.

port_angeles.title: Butterfly Season at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: Mid-July is peak butterfly season at Hurricane Ridge! Look for colorful Western Tiger Swallowtails, Painted Ladies, and Arctic Fritillaries dancing among the wildflowers. The alpine meadows are alive with these delicate visitors enjoying the mountain sunshine.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right! Amazing how fast those tiny hearts beat! 🐦
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is about 1,200 beats per minute — incredibly fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are incredibly fast — about 1,200 beats per minute during flight! That's more than 20 beats every second.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this incredible heart rate, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in nectar each day. They visit hundreds of flowers daily to keep their energy up!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Igen! Pillangó is butterfly — such a lovely, musical word! 🦋
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is pillangó! Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee. Hungarian has such beautiful words for nature.
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' is thought to be onomatopoetic, mimicking the light fluttering sound of butterfly wings. Many Hungarian nature words have similarly poetic origins.

puzzle_3.question: What tree species dominates the old-growth forests of Olympic National Park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! Sitka Spruce trees are the giants of Olympic's rainforests, some reaching over 300 feet tall! 🌲
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the Sitka Spruce! These magnificent trees thrive in the wet coastal climate of the Olympic Peninsula and can live for over 700 years, creating the magical rainforest cathedral feeling.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Hoh and Quinault rainforests contain some of the largest Sitka Spruces in the world. These trees can grow over 10 feet in diameter and create ecosystems that support hundreds of other species.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
